expectations.evidence: Lines 56–63: `acceptedDocuments` is set to `[DriverLicense(region: .us)]` correctly, but the model also sets `rejectedDocuments` to three additional document types, going beyond the required configuration.
No `rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes` assignment anywhere in the file. The model used `AamvaBarcodeVerifier` via the old verifier-based API instead.
No `rejectInconsistentData` assignment anywhere in the file. Consistency checking is attempted through `AamvaVizBarcodeComparisonVerifier` via the deprecated verifier path.

expectations.text: Calls settings.setIncludeImage(true, for: .croppedDocument) — required for frontReviewImage to be populated
expectations.evidence: No call to `setIncludeImage(_:for:)` anywhere in the file. Without this, `frontReviewImage` will always be nil.

expectations.text: Does NOT use AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier — verification is settings-driven
expectations.evidence: Lines 66–72: instantiates `AamvaVizBarcodeComparisonVerifier()` and `AamvaBarcodeVerifier()` and passes them to `USDLVerificationSettings(verifiers:)`, which is the deprecated verifier-based approach.
Lines 209–211: accesses `capturedId.usdlVerificationResult?.frontReviewImage` — uses the wrong property path (`usdlVerificationResult` instead of `verificationResult.dataConsistency`).

expectations.text: Handles .inconsistentData, .forgedAamvaBarcode, and .documentExpired in didReject with distinct messages
expectations.evidence: Line 208 handles `.inconsistentData`. There is no `case .forgedAamvaBarcode` branch. Line 204 uses `.expired` (not `.documentExpired`), which is a different enum value. Two of the three required cases are absent or wrong.

expectations.text: Uses idCapture.reset() when re-enabling capture after showing the verification result (or correctly re-enables isEnabled)
expectations.evidence: `idCapture.isEnabled = true` is set in the completion block of every `showAlert` and `displayReviewImage` call throughout didCapture and didReject, correctly re-enabling capture after dismissal.
